text:
Once upon a time in a small forest nearby a quaint town named Willowbrook, there lived a wood owl named Orb. Orb lived alone most nights. Every morning he’d gaze southward towards the town at dawn. One day, curiosity got the better of him. He swooped down, landed on a ancient oak, and peered into a small window. He saw children playing. Laughter? Intrigued, he stayed there, watching.

Hours went by, and after feeling warm and full of sunshine, he fluffed his feathers. Suddenly the window shut quietly. Orb hooted softly. Inside was Mrs. Jenkins, knitting.

“Oh dear,” she said with a laugh. “Didn’t think anyone was there.”

Not wanting to scare her, Orb fluttered down. She gave him some carrot slices she had saved. They chatted for a while.

“Do you come here often?” she asked.

“It’s pleasant,” Orb replied.

She told him all about her days knitting wool
